role: This file is the tldraw custom node shape, graph-to-canvas synchronization layer, and screen-space edge overlay for the visual graph editor.
capabilities: Defines a generic 'node' tldraw shape with fixed dimensions and disabled resize/rotate
Renders a node card with status dot, node type, kind, address, and semantic-zoom body
Reads port schemas and status definitions from the registry store at render time
Renders left input and right output port nubs at vertically computed positions
Implements pointer-based output-to-input connection drag using pointer capture and elementFromPoint
Commits new connections via the registry CONNECT action
Provides a force re-run control that calls FORCE_RUN for a node
Loads the backend graph into tldraw by creating, updating, or pruning shapes while preserving backend positions
Refreshes node status, output, layer, and error props without a full reload
Renders a reactive SVG edge overlay that anchors wires to exact source/target ports in screen space
Supports semantic zoom by collapsing/expanding the node body based on zoom level
Attaches data-ui-ref live addresses and portal-window addresses for resolver integration
substance_why: It fully implements node rendering, port-based connection gestures, backend graph load/refresh/prune, and a reactive SVG edge overlay with no TODOs or placeholder stubs.
interactions_present: Drag from an output nub to an input nub to create a connection
Click the force re-run icon on a node's address bar
Hover port nubs to see input/output titles
Semantic zoom expands or collapses the node body automatically
Standard tldraw selection and drag of node shapes
Display of portal live/empty/ref state in the node body
interactions_missing: No delete-node or create-node interaction in the canvas
No editing of node config, ref, or address from the card
No visual preview wire while dragging a connection
No keyboard or accessible alternatives for connection or force-run
No user-facing error feedback if CONNECT or FORCE_RUN fails
No context menu, pan-to-node, or node inspection trigger from the card
performance_risks: Edges overlay recomputes every line on each camera/zoom change via useValue, doing O(n) shape lookups and pageToScreen calls
Direct registry store reads in component render (getOINFO/getNODE_STATES) re-evaluate every render without memoization
loadGraph and refresh call editor.updateShape per node sequentially, potentially causing many tldraw transactions
document.elementFromPoint in onPointerUp performs a synchronous DOM hit-test that may collide with overlay elements
Edges list uses array index as React key, which can cause unnecessary re-mounts when edge order changes
No throttling or debouncing of refresh/loadGraph calls
reuse_why: The node shape semantics, port anchoring, and backend-sync logic are worth preserving, but the monolithic file and unoptimized rendering should be refactored during redesign.
decomposition_hints: Split graph synchronization (loadGraph/refresh) from the shape renderer
Extract the Edges overlay and its geometry computation into a dedicated component/hook
Extract port geometry helpers and the Nub component into a separate module
Extract status/error/body rendering into a NodeBody subcomponent
Separate connection gesture handling (drag/drop) from the node card rendering
Introduce adapter hooks for registry store reads to isolate tldraw components from direct store access
